title: Aureline security advisory record
description: Boundary schema for the advisory_record frozen in docs/security/severity_matrix.md. One record carries the stable Aureline advisory identity, the CVE and GHSA aliases reserved alongside it, the advisory subject kind, the affected-subject refs, signer / source state, affected-install linkage (install-profile cards, exact-build identity refs, channel classes, publication posture, mirror freshness, local continuity), disclosure class, deployment-profile scope, evidence refs with typed embedding state, and linkage hooks into the shared emergency-action / revocation object model plus the still-deferred disable-bundle payload family. Raw binary bytes, raw signing material, raw dependency source, raw exploit payloads, raw evidence bodies, and raw reporter identities never cross this boundary; the record carries opaque refs, typed vocabulary, and redaction-class metadata only. The eventual security-response crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this file is the cross-tool boundary every public advisory, release-note, About / Help surface, support-bundle export, release-evidence packet, and revocation / disable-bundle record reads. Adding a new security_severity_class, advisory_subject_kind, mirror_freshness_class, embedding_state, disclosure_class, or publication_posture value is additive-minor and requires an advisory_record_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row co-signed by security_trust_review and release_council.

advisory_record_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this advisory record export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator. Every advisory carries record_kind = advisory_record so parity audits and support exports can validate payloads mechanically.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles / claim manifests / release-evidence packets. Raw binary bytes, raw signing material, raw dependency source, raw evidence bodies, and raw reporter identities never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short, reviewable sentence describing a posture or note. Raw secret material and raw exploit payloads MUST NOT appear.

security_severity_class.description: Frozen severity vocabulary. See docs/security/severity_matrix.md 'Severity vocabulary' for owner, response-clock, minimum-review-forum, and documentation / update obligation per level. A record whose severity cannot be resolved fails closed with severity_class_unresolved rather than defaulting.

advisory_subject_kind.description: Frozen advisory-subject-kind vocabulary. A record whose subject cannot be typed fails closed with subject_kind_unresolved rather than defaulting. Adding a new value is additive-minor and requires a schema version bump.

cve_id_string.description: CVE identifier in the form CVE-YYYY-NNNN+. Year is four digits; sequence is at least four digits. The record carries a null cve_id until a CVE is assigned.

ghsa_id_string.description: GHSA identifier in the form GHSA-xxxx-yyyy-zzzz. Lower-case alphanumeric segments. The record carries a null ghsa_id until a GHSA is minted.

aureline_advisory_id_string.description: Stable Aureline advisory id in the form AURELINE-ADV-YYYY-NNNN+. Year is four digits; sequence is at least four digits. Stable for the life of the advisory even when the finding is reclassified.

cve_id.description: CVE alias. Null when no CVE has been assigned. Populated in place, not by minting a new advisory id.

ghsa_id.description: GHSA alias. Null when no GHSA has been minted. Populated in place, not by minting a new advisory id.

additional_alias_refs.description: Reserved opaque alias refs for future disclosure schemes. Empty at this milestone; later alias classes land under this array rather than adding a new top-level field.

advisory_identity.description: Advisory identity envelope. aureline_advisory_id is required on every advisory; cve_id and ghsa_id are nullable alias slots that downstream disclosure, revocation, and disable-bundle artifacts resolve against so no parallel identity system is minted. additional_alias_refs is reserved so later schemes (for example OSV or a coordinated-disclosure group's id format) attach without a top-level schema change.

affected_subject_ref.description: Typed reference to an affected subject. Raw subject bodies never appear here.

signing_class.description: Frozen signing-class vocabulary. Mirrors the exact-build identity model (schemas/build/exact_build_identity.schema.json) so an advisory that names a build resolves into the same signing vocabulary the build itself carries.

signing_material_state_class.description: Frozen signing-material state vocabulary. Mirrors the exact-build identity model. signed_revoked is a publishable blocker on every downstream surface.

signing_authority_ref.description: Opaque ref to the signing authority the affected subject was signed by. Null when the subject was unsigned_development_only or when the signing authority is not known.

transparency_log_ref.description: Opaque ref to the transparency-log entry for the subject's signature. Null when the subject is unsigned or when the signing class does not require a transparency-log anchor.

signer_source_state.description: Signer / source state for the affected subject. The record names the signing class and signing-material state the subject was published under and carries an opaque ref to the signing authority plus a transparency-log ref when applicable. Raw signing material never appears here.

release_channel_class.description: Frozen release-channel vocabulary. Mirrors schemas/build/exact_build_identity.schema.json so an advisory resolves the same channel vocabulary the exact-build identity carries.

publication_posture_class.description: Frozen publication-posture vocabulary. Mirrors docs/release/install_topology_plan.md 'Mirror / air-gap publication posture'.

mirror_freshness_class.description: Frozen mirror-freshness vocabulary. A mirror that does not carry the fix is stale_within_grace (fix is recent, grace window applies), stale_past_grace (grace window elapsed), offline_expired (air-gap bundle past its offline_expiration_at), or unknown (the freshness cannot be resolved). up_to_date means the mirror or local cache already carries the fix.

install_profile_card_refs.description: Opaque refs into artifacts/release/install_topology_matrix.yaml. Each ref names one install_profile_card_record the advisory applies to. A critical or high advisory MUST NOT carry an empty list.

exact_build_identity_refs.description: Opaque refs into schemas/build/exact_build_identity.schema.json (exact_build_identity_ref values). Each ref names one exact-build identity the advisory applies to. The record MUST carry at least one ref — free-text 'version 1.x' is non-conforming.

channel_classes.description: Release channel classes the advisory applies to. Drawn from the exact-build identity model's vocabulary.

publication_posture_classes.description: Publication postures the advisory applies to. An advisory that affects every posture lists every value.

offline_expiration_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when a currently-shipped offline bundle containing the affected subject expires without a fix. Null when the advisory does not carry an offline / air-gapped scope.

affected_install_linkage.description: Affected-install assessment linkage. Names the install-profile cards, exact-build identities, channels, publication postures, and mirror-freshness class the advisory applies to, plus a local-continuity note describing what still works locally if the affected install is disabled before the fix is available. An operational-emergency, critical, or high advisory with an empty install_profile_card_refs or empty exact_build_identity_refs is non-conforming at the record level.

deployment_profile_class.description: Frozen deployment-profile vocabulary. Mirrors docs/product/boundary_manifest_strawman.md so an advisory resolves into the same deployment-profile ids the boundary manifest uses.

disclosure_class.description: Frozen disclosure-class vocabulary. Mirrors artifacts/governance/issue_routing.yaml disclosure_classes. security_issue reports default to public_on_advisory; downgrading to private_indefinite is a governance decision recorded in decision_history[].

public_summary_state.description: Frozen public-summary-state vocabulary. not_required applies only at security_severity.low. pending_* states cover drafting, review, and publication gates. published is the published state. withdrawn is the explicit-retraction state and requires a decision-history row.

embedding_state.description: Frozen evidence-embedding vocabulary. See docs/security/severity_matrix.md 'Evidence embedding vocabulary' for semantics. An evidence item that asserts embedded but carries no body, redacted but no redaction-pass class, or by_reference but no reference_ref, is non-conforming.

redaction_class.description: Frozen redaction-class vocabulary for this record. log_safe / support_export_only / evidence_packet_only / release_public mirror the redaction envelope pinned in schemas/build/exact_build_identity.schema.json; private_triage_only is admissible only when the advisory's evidence is carried inside a named private-triage workspace scope and is not otherwise exported. release_public is the only class that may appear verbatim in public release notes or About / Help dialogs.

redaction_pass_class.description: Frozen redaction-pass-class vocabulary. Names which redaction pass ran before the evidence body was embedded. broker_default_* values inherit the redaction defaults frozen in schemas/security/secret_class_rows.schema.json (ADR-0007). private_triage_manual_review applies only inside a private-triage workspace scope.

evidence_id.description: Opaque, stable id for the evidence item. Stable across a redaction-pass rerun; different passes do not mint new ids.

embedded_payload_ref.description: Opaque ref to the embedded payload. Non-null only when embedding_state is embedded or redacted; null otherwise. Raw payloads never appear in this record; the ref resolves through the evidence resolver under the redaction envelope named on redaction_class.

reference_ref.description: Opaque ref to the artefact that carries the body when embedding_state is by_reference (support bundle, release-evidence packet, private-triage workspace note). Null when embedding_state is embedded, redacted, or omitted.

redaction_pass_class.description: Which redaction pass ran before the evidence body was embedded. Non-null when embedding_state is redacted; null for embedded (no redaction applied, admissible only for non-secret classes), by_reference, and omitted.

placeholder_shape.description: Short description of the placeholder that stands in for every redacted span (for example '<redacted: ai_provider_token>'). Non-null when embedding_state is redacted; null otherwise.

allOf.description: embedding_state = embedded or redacted requires embedded_payload_ref.
embedding_state = redacted requires redaction_pass_class and placeholder_shape.
embedding_state = omitted forbids embedded_payload_ref and reference_ref.

evidence_item.description: One evidence item referenced by the advisory. Raw bodies never appear; the item carries an embedding state, an opaque ref to the body (embedded, by-reference) or no ref at all (omitted), a redaction-pass class when the body was embedded after redaction, and a placeholder shape describing what redacted spans look like in the embedded body.

actor_ref.description: Opaque ref to the actor (maintainer pseudonym, forum chair, co-signer). Raw reporter identities never appear here.

forum.description: Forum the decision event is recorded against. Mirrors artifacts/governance/ownership_matrix.yaml decision_forums[]. Operational-emergency, critical, and high advisories MUST record at least one entry with forum: security_trust_review and at least one co-sign entry with forum: release_council.

outcome.description: Frozen outcome vocabulary for decision-history entries. severity_upgraded / severity_downgraded are the only mechanisms by which severity_class changes; there is no backdating.

decision_history_entry.description: One decision-history entry. The entry is append-only; prior entries never mutate. A severity change without a corresponding severity_upgraded / severity_downgraded entry is non-conforming.

trust_state.description: ADR-0009 trust state of the producing context.

policy_context.description: Policy-context envelope re-exported from ADR-0008 / ADR-0009 so downstream lanes can reason about the advisory's policy provenance without re-deriving it.

title.description: Short, reviewable advisory title. Never carries raw secret material or raw exploit payload. Used as the public-advisory heading and the About / Help notice heading.

affected_subjects.description: Typed refs to every subject the advisory applies to. At least one entry is required.

deployment_profile_scope.description: Deployment profiles the advisory applies to. Drawn from the boundary-manifest deployment-profile vocabulary.

public_summary_ref.description: Opaque ref to the published public-summary artefact (release note row, advisory page, coordinated-disclosure summary). Non-null when public_summary_state is published; null otherwise. A security_severity.medium, security_severity.high, security_severity.critical, or security_severity.operational_emergency advisory with a null public_summary_ref at public_summary_state = published is non-conforming.

monitored_contact_ref.description: Opaque ref to the monitored SECURITY.md contact path (security_md_monitored_contact). See docs/security/severity_matrix.md 'Monitored contact path'. An advisory with an unresolved monitored_contact_ref fails closed; publication is blocked.

private_triage_workspace_ref.description: Opaque ref to the private-triage workspace the advisory was triaged in. Resolves into an incident_workspace_packet_record (schemas/security/incident_workspace_packet.schema.json). An advisory with an unresolved private_triage_workspace_ref fails closed.

evidence.description: Evidence items referenced by the advisory. Each item names its own embedding state (omitted / embedded / redacted / by_reference) and, when applicable, the redaction-pass class that ran before the body was embedded. Raw secret material MUST NOT appear; placeholders stand in.

emergency_action_refs.description: Opaque refs to emergency_action_record ids in schemas/security/emergency_action_record.schema.json. Emergency actions reuse the same affected-install linkage and exact-build identity model instead of minting a parallel security-response identity system.

revocation_refs.description: Opaque refs to revocation_record ids in schemas/security/emergency_action_record.schema.json. Revocations remain on the same artifact-identity graph as the advisory and any linked emergency action.

disable_bundle_refs.description: Reserved opaque refs to disable-bundle artifacts linked to this advisory. The disable-bundle artefact is a later lane.

decision_history.description: Append-only decision history. Every severity reclassification, mitigation event, advisory-publication event, revocation event, and emergency-action invocation lands here. Single-forum close on security_severity.operational_emergency / security_severity.critical / security_severity.high is non-conforming: both security_trust_review and release_council MUST appear in the history.

allOf.description: security_severity.low advisories MUST NOT carry emergency_action_refs, revocation_refs, or disable_bundle_refs entries.

security_severity.operational_emergency, security_severity.critical, and security_severity.high advisories MUST carry a non-empty affected_install_linkage.install_profile_card_refs and a non-empty affected_install_linkage.exact_build_identity_refs.
public_summary_state = not_required is admissible only at security_severity.low.
Operational-emergency, critical, and high advisories require both security_trust_review and release_council entries on the decision trail.
public_summary_state = published requires a non-null public_summary_ref.

advisory_record.description: Advisory record. One record per advisory. The record carries the Aureline advisory id plus CVE and GHSA alias slots, the advisory subject kind and affected subject refs, signer / source state, affected-install linkage into the install-topology matrix and exact-build identity model, deployment-profile scope, disclosure class, public-summary state, monitored contact ref, private-triage workspace ref, evidence refs with typed embedding state, linkage hooks into the shared emergency-action / revocation object model plus the deferred disable-bundle family, append-only decision history, policy context, and redaction class. Downstream public advisories, release notes, About / Help notices, support-bundle exports, and release-evidence packets resolve a single advisory_identity.aureline_advisory_id into one record — no parallel identity system.
